title: Shivkhori
tagline: Shivkhori — spiritual town of Jammu & Kashmir

overview.short: Shiv Khori, is a famous cave shrines of Hindus devoted to lord Shiva, situated in the Sangar village, Pouni, near Reasi town in the Reasi district of Jammu and Kashmir in India.

topPlaces.name: Siar Baba Waterfall
topPlaces.description: Siar Baba Waterfall is a waterfall near the Siar Baba temple, situated on the Chenab River in Kotla village of Reasi district, Jammu and Kashmir which comes under the region of Jammu division. The Siar Baba Waterfall is around 52.8 kilometres (32.8 mi) away from Reasi town towards south via NH144, 109 kilometres (68 mi) from Jammu Airport towards south via NH144, and 106 kilometres (66 mi) from Tawi River by crossing the Katra and Vaishno Devi.

items.note: Siar Baba Waterfall is a waterfall near the Siar Baba temple, situated on the Chenab River in Kotla village of Reasi district, Jammu and Kashmir which comes under the region of Jammu division. The Siar Baba Waterfall is around 52.8 kilometres (32.8 mi) away from Reasi town towards south via NH144, 109 kilometres (68 mi) from Jammu Airport towards south via NH144, and 106 kilometres (66 mi) from Tawi River by crossing the Katra and Vaishno Devi.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Shivkhori?
faq.a: The best time to visit Shivkhori is Mar – Jun, Sep – Nov. Summers are 15–28°C and winters are 2–16°C.

The nearest airport is Jammu Airport (58 km) and the nearest railway station is Katra (Vaishno Devi railhead) (38 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Shivkhori?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Shivkhori at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Shivkhori?
faq.a: Stays in Shivkhori start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Shivkhori?
faq.a: The top attractions are Siar Baba Waterfall.
